content:
From Chat Apps to Live Dashboards — Authentication, Scaling, Streaming, and Production Deployment

9 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Real-time communication used to require complex WebSocket management, connection state tracking, and fallback logic for browsers that didn’t support the latest protocols. SignalR abstracts all of that into a clean Hub API where you call methods on clients as if they were local objects.
In .NET 10, SignalR is more mature than ever — with built-in streaming, JWT authentication, Redis backplane support, and native integration with Azure SignalR Service for scaling to millions of concurrent connections. This article shows you the complete implementation: a production-ready notification hub with authentication, streaming, groups, and horizontal scaling.
What You’re Building
Part 1: The Server — Hub with Authentication
SignalR Hubs are the server-side endpoints that handle client connections, receive method calls, and push messages to connected clients.
